sections.paragraph:
Ἕλλην, ηνος, ὁ (s. Ἑλλάς; Hdt.+)

① a pers. of Greek language and culture, Greek (opp. βάρβαρος [ANikolaidis, Ἑλληνικός–βαρβαρικός, Plutarch on Greek and Barbarian Characteristics: WienerStud n.s. 20, ’86, 229–44] as Thu. 1, 1, 2 et al.; TestSol 6:8 PL; Philo, Ebr. 193 al.; Jos., Ant. 4, 12 al.; Just., D. 117, 5; Tat. 21, 3; s. UWilcken, Hellenen u. Barbaren: NJklA 17, 1906, 457–71; JJüthner, Hell. u. Bar. 1923; HRiesenfeld, ConNeot 9, ’44, 1ff) Ro 1:14 (cultured Romans affected interest in things Greek and would therefore recognize themselves under this term).

② in the broader sense, all persons who came under the influence of Greek, as distinguished from Israel’s, culture

ⓐ gentile, polytheist, Greco-Roman (2 Macc 4:36; 11:2; 3 Macc 3:8; 4 Macc 18:20; ViJon 1 [p. 82, 15 Sch.]; SibOr 5, 265; Praxagoras [Hist. IV a.d.]: 219 T 1, 8 [Phot., Bibl. 62, 8:219] Jac. τὴν θρησκείαν Ἕλλην; apolog.) J 7:35; Ac 9:29 v.l. and 11:20 v.l. (both for Ἑλληνιστάς); 16:1, 3; 21:28; 1 Cor 1:22; Gal 2:3; PtK 2 p. 14, 1 and 7; p. 15, 7; Dg 1; 3:3; 5:17. The expr. Ἰουδαῖοι καὶ Ἕλληνες, which clearly indicates Israel’s advantages fr. Judean perspective, embraces a broad range of nationalities, with Ἕλλην focusing on the polytheistic aspect: Ac 14:1; 18:4; 19:10, 17; 20:21; Ro 1:16; 2:9f; 3:9; 10:12; 1 Cor 1:24; 10:32; 12:13; Gal 3:28; Col 3:11 (CPJ 33, 6f=Mitt-Wilck. 55, 6 [III b.c.] παρὰ τῶν Ἰουδαίων καὶ τῶν Ἑλλήνων does not mean to indicate that Judeans have any special privilege [despite the fact that Ἰ. comes before Ἑ.]. Here the expression expresses gentile perspective. Cp. also Epict. 2, 9, 19 τί ὑποκρίνῃ Ἰουδαῖον ὢν Ἕλλην=why do you play the part of a Judean, when you are actually a Greek?).

ⓑ used of non-Israelites/gentiles who expressed an interest in the cultic life of Israel (cp. Jos., Bell. 7, 45) J 12:20. οἱ σεβόμενοι Ἕλληνες God-fearing gentiles Ac 17:4.—On the use of the art. s. B-D-F §262, 2=Rehkopf §262, 3.—LWeniger, Jesus u. d. Griechen: NJklA 41, 1918, 477–80; JLeipoldt, Jesu Verh. zu Griech. u. Juden ’41; B. 1489.—Frisk s.v. Ἑλλάς. DELG s.v. Ἕλληνες. M-M. EDNT. TW. Sv.

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

pers. pers. = person(s)

opp. opp. = opposed to, opposite

n.s. n.s. = new series (also used to render Neue Folge)

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

NJklA NJklA = Neue Jahrbücher für das klassische Altertum—List 6

ConNeot ConNeot = Coniectanea Neotestamentica—List 6

Sch. Sch. = TSchermann, Prophetarum vitae fabulosae—List 5

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

Hist.

Hist. (after a personal name)=historicus, historian

hist. = historian, historical

Phot Phot , IX a.d.—List 5

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

apolog. apolog. = apologist(s)

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

PtK PtK = Petruskerygma (Preaching of Peter)—List 1

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

expr. expr. = expression

  1. fr. = from

CPJ CPJ = Corpus Papyrorum Judaicarum—List 4

Mitt-Wilck. Mitt-Wilck. = LMitteis/UWilcken, Grundzüge und Chrestomathie der Papyruskunde—Lists 4, 6

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

art. art. = article

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

Frisk Frisk = HF., Griechisches etymologisches Wörterbuch—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

EDNT EDNT = Exegetical Dictionary of the NT—List 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 318
